Ukraine Conflict News





25 December 2025 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • Trump confirms upcoming start of ground strikes against drug cartel targets in Latin America People's Daily 25 Dec 2025 -- U.S. President Donald Trump confirmed on Wednesday the upcoming start of ground strikes against drug cartel targets in Latin America.
  • White House orders U.S. military to focus on "quarantine" of Venezuelan oil: media People's Daily 25 Dec 2025 -- The White House has ordered the U.S. military to focus almost exclusively on enforcing a "quarantine" of Venezuelan oil for at least the next two months, Reuters reported on Wednesday, citing a U.S. official.

  • Trump Confirms Upcoming Start of Ground Strikes Against Drug Cartels in Latin America Sputnik 25 Dec 2025 -- US President Donald Trump confirmed the upcoming start of ground strikes against drug cartel targets in Latin America.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Su-35S Tops Russian Fighters in Targets Destroyed in Ukraine - Rostec Sputnik 25 Dec 2025 -- The Su-35S fighter has emerged as one of Russia's most in-demand combat jets, destroying the highest number of targets during the special military operation in Ukraine, Russian state tech corporation Rostec said on Thursday.
  • Soyuz-2.1a Rocket Launched From Plesetsk Cosmodrome - Russian Defense Ministry Sputnik 25 Dec 2025 -- The Russian space forces successfully launched a Soyuz-2.1a rocket carrying a satellite from the Plesetsk Cosmodrome in the Arkhangelsk Region, the Russian Defense Ministry said on Thursday.

  • Ukrainian businesses thank service members: the 'Pluses' Program delivers over UAH 500 million in savings in its first year Ukraine MOD 25 Dec 2025
  • Tactical reconnaissance and strike missions: key facts about the Ukrainian Linza UAV to be co-produced with Germany Ukraine MOD 25 Dec 2025
  • The Ministry of Defence implements photo and video documentation for quality control of non-lethal supplies Ukraine MOD 25 Dec 2025

  • Regular Press Conference of the Ministry of National Defense on December 25, 2025 PRC MND 25 Dec 2025
    • Pentagon's hype of China loading 100 ICBMs nothing but an attempt to find excuse to expand its own nuclear forces: MND spokesperson Global Times 25 Dec 2025 -- Responding to a draft Pentagon report which claimed that China has likely loaded more than 100 ICBMs in three silo fields, a spokesperson for China's Ministry of National Defense (MND) on Thursday said that the US, instead of reflecting on its own actions, hypes up the so-called "China nuclear threat," which is nothing but an attempt to divert international attention and find excuses for accelerating the expansion of its own nuclear forces.
  • Chinese Defense Ministry spokesperson slams Pentagon's report, urges US to develop correct understanding of China Global Times 25 Dec 2025 -- In response to the 2025 Military and Security Developments involving the People's Republic of China recently released by the US Department of Defense, Chinese Defense Ministry spokesperson Zhang Xiaogang said at a regular press conference on Thursday that the US issues such reports year after year, brazenly interfering in China's internal affairs, maliciously distorting China's defense policy, groundlessly speculating about the development of China's military capabilities, and slandering the normal operations of the Chinese armed forces.
  • Defense ministry denounces U.S. report hyping "China military threat" PRC MND 25 Dec 2025 -- A Chinese defense spokesperson on Thursday expressed strong dissatisfaction and firm opposition to a recent U.S. report that misrepresents China's national defense policy and military development.
  • "Falcon Shield 2025" joint air force training promotes practical cooperation between China and UAE: Defense Spokesperson PRC MND 25 Dec 2025 -- "The 'Falcon Shield' joint training is a signature project of cooperation between the air forces of China and the United Arab Emirates. So far, it has been held three times and has played an important role in promoting practical cooperation between the two sides and maintaining regional peace," said Senior Colonel Zhang Xiaogang, spokesperson for China's Ministry of National Defense, at a press conference on Thursday.
  • PLA's J-10, KJ-500, YU-20A aircraft conduct joint air force training in UAE for 1st time: defense ministry spokesperson Global Times 25 Dec 2025 -- The J-10C fighter jet, KJ-500 early warning aircraft and YU-20A tanker aircraft of the PLA Air Force for the first time participated in a joint training in the UAE, Chinese Defense Ministry spokesperson Zhang Xiaogang said on Thursday. Zhang said that the China-UAE joint air force training Falcon Shield 2025 was conducted from December 9 to Monday.
  • Burst power of electromagnetic catapult on China's aircraft carrier Fujian shown in official media program Global Times 25 Dec 2025 -- A recent official Chinese media program provided a detailed up-close look at the electromagnetic aircraft launch system on the newly commissioned aircraft carrier Fujian, showing its high burst power.

  • Press Statement of Spokesperson for DPRK Ministry of National Defence KCNA 25 Dec 2025 -- A spokesperson for the Ministry of National Defence of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea released the following press statement on December 24:
  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un Guides Building of Nuclear-powered Strategic Guided Missile Submarine KCNA 25 Dec 2025 -- Kim Jong Un , general secretary of the Workers' Party of Korea and president of the State Affairs of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea, guided the building of an 8 700-tonnage nuclear-powered strategic guided missile submarine on the spot.
  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un Observes Test-fire of New-type Anti-Air Missiles KCNA 25 Dec 2025 -- Kim Jong Un , general secretary of the Workers' Party of Korea and president of the State Affairs of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea, observed a test-fire of new-type anti-air missiles.
  • North Korea Advances Nuclear Deterrent with New Strategic Submarine — Report Sputnik 25 Dec 2025 -- Kim Jong Un guided the construction of an 8,700-ton nuclear-powered strategic missile submarine, the Korean Central News Agency (KCNA) reported.

  • His Excellency Major General Yousef Ahmed A. Al Huneiti, Chairman Joint Chiefs of Staff (CJCS) of the Jordan Armed Forces, called on Field Marshal Syed Asim Munir, NI (M), HJ, COAS & CDF, at General Headquarters (GHQ), Rawalpindi. ISPR Pakistan 25 Dec 2025

  • MND: Japan's Offensive Space Policy is Extremely Dangerous Global Times 25 Dec 2025
  • MND: Make United Efforts to Contain the Revival of Militarism by Japan's Right-wing Forces Global Times 25 Dec 2025 -- Question: According to media reports, regarding the introduction of nuclear-powered submarines, Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi said: "We will not rule out all options". An official from the Prime Minister's Office stated that Japan should possess nuclear weapons.
  • Japan's pursuit for militarization poses threat to peace, stability in region and beyond: Defense Spokesperson PRC MND 25 Dec 2025 -- "We call upon all peace-loving nations and peoples to take actions to resolutely contain the revival of militarism by Japan's right-wing forces, and prevent the world from sliding into turmoil or reliving tragedies of the past," said Senior Colonel Zhang Xiaogang, spokesperson for China's Ministry of National Defense, at a regular press conference on Thursday.
  • Appeasement to Japan's right-wing forces will inevitably lead to a backlash: Defense Spokesperson PRC MND 25 Dec 2025 -- "Japan openly abandoned its obligations under international law and violated the provisions of its own Constitution and political commitments by substantially increasing its defense budget, and engaging in military expansion and war preparations. Where is Japan's credibility? And what does Japan intend to do?" asked Senior Colonel Zhang Xiaogang, spokesperson for China's Ministry of National Defense, at a press conference on Thursday.
  • China's defense ministry calls for united efforts to contain Japan's militarism revival PRC MND 25 Dec 2025 -- China's defense ministry on Thursday called on all peace-loving nations and peoples to take united actions to contain the revival of militarism by Japan's right-wing forces.
  • Japan's offensive space policy is extremely dangerous: spokesperson PRC MND 25 Dec 2025 -- A Chinese military spokesperson on Thursday said that Japan's unrestrained development of satellite-jamming technology is accelerating the weaponization and militarization of space, and fueling a space arms race, which is extremely dangerous and unpopular.
  • MND spokesperson slams Japan's offensive space policy as 'extremely dangerous,' urging peace-loving countries to contain right-wing militarism Global Times 25 Dec 2025 -- China's Ministry of National Defense spokesperson slammed Japan's offensive space policy as extremely dangerous, and called on peace-loving countries and peoples to take action to resolutely contain the revival of militarism by Japan's right-wing forces and prevent the world from sliding into turmoil or repeating the tragedies of the past.
  • Japanese media unveils PM's non-proliferation aide outed as official behind nuclear weapons possession claim Global Times 25 Dec 2025 -- Japanese media Shūkan Bunshun reported on Wednesday that the anonymous official from the Prime Minister's Office cited in reports claiming that "Japan should possess nuclear weapons", a statement that drew widespread condemnation, is Oue Sadamasa, a special advisor to Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, whose portfolio includes nuclear disarmament and non-proliferation.
  • Japanese official's remarks about possessing nuclear weapons not isolated incident: FM spokesperson People's Daily 25 Dec 2025 -- Chinese foreign ministry on Wednesday continued to blast the widely-criticized remarks by a senior Japanese official that "Japan should have nuclear weapons," saying it was by no means an isolated incident.

  • The Chief of the General Staff at the Intelligence Directorate's Research Department Conference Israel Defense Forces 25 Dec 2025

  • IAEA Sees No Signs of Countries Preparing to Resume Nuclear Testing - Grossi Sputnik 25 Dec 2025 -- The International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) sees no signs of nuclear powers preparing to resume nuclear testing, IAEA Director Rafael Grossi said in an interview with Sputnik.

  • France condemns U.S. visa ban on ex-EU commissioner People's Daily 25 Dec 2025 -- France on Wednesday strongly condemned visa restrictions imposed by the United States on Thierry Breton, former European Commissioner for the Internal Market, and four other European figures.

  • Secretary Rubio's Call with Cambodian Prime Minister Hun Manet US Dept. of State 25 Dec 2025

Defense Industry

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

    Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • Israeli war minister vows to continue aggression against Gaza, three Arab countries Press TV 25 Dec 2025 -- Israeli minister of military affairs, Israel Katz, has pledged to continue military assaults across the region, including Gaza, where mass civilian casualties and an aid blockade have fueled one of the worst humanitarian crises in decades.

  • More Than Four Kilometers of Underground Tunnel Routes Dismantled and Dozens of Terrorists Eliminated: Activity of the IDF Northern Gaza Brigade Israel Defense Forces 25 Dec 2025

  • Health system nears 'complete paralysis' as Israel uses medicine as weapon: Gaza officials Press TV 25 Dec 2025 -- Gaza officials have warned of an impending humanitarian and medical catastrophe in the territory, saying Israel is deliberately restricting the entry of vital medicines, medical equipment, and fuel, using healthcare as a weapon against civilians.
  • Israeli military launches new strikes across Gaza amid systematic ceasefire violations Press TV 25 Dec 2025 -- Israel has unleashed new airstrikes and artillery fire across Gaza, continuing to violate the ceasefire while choking off desperately needed humanitarian aid to the war-torn coastal region.

  • Hamas official: 'Land grab part of Israel's ethnic cleansing policy' Press TV 25 Dec 2025 -- A senior Hamas official has warned about the escalation of Israel's land confiscations across the occupied West Bank, saying it is an extension of the regime's ethnic cleansing policy.

  • Israeli attacks kill 3 Lebanese in east, south Lebanon as ceasefire violations mount Press TV 25 Dec 2025 -- Israeli drone attacks across southern and eastern Lebanon have killed at least three people, as the regime pressed on with strikes, ground incursions, and airspace violations in an open defiance of UN resolutions and Lebanon's sovereignty.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Anti-Taliban Figure Ikramuddin Saree Killed In Iran RFE/RL 25 Dec 2025 -- Ikramuddin Saree, a former police commander in Afghanistan's Takhar and Baghlan provinces under the pre-Taliban republic, was shot dead on the evening of December 24 outside his office in Tehran.

  • General Secretariat Expresses Concern Regarding Developments in Yemen Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 25 Dec 2025 -- The General Secretariat of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) has expressed profound concern about the recent unilateral military mobilizations by the Southern Transitional Council in the Hadramawt and Al-Mahra governorates of Yemen

  • AUSSOM lauds UN Security Council's renewal of mandate amid fight against terrorism People's Daily 25 Dec 2025 -- The African Union Support and Stabilization Mission in Somalia (AUSSOM) on Wednesday welcomed the UN Security Council's decision to renew its mandate for another year.

  • On 24 December 2025, Security Forces conducted an intelligence based operation in general area Kulachi, Dera Ismail Khan District on reported presence of Khwarij. ISPR Pakistan 25 Dec 2025

  • On 24 December 2025, security forces conducted an intelligence based operation in Kalat District of Balochistan, on reported presence of terrorists belonging to Indian proxy, Fitna al Hindustan. ISPR Pakistan 25 Dec 2025

  • Statement by the Nigeria Resident and Humanitarian Coordinator, Mohamed Fall, on loss of lives in a suspected suicide attack in Gamboru, Maiduguri, Borno State UNOCHA 25 Dec 2025 -- I am deeply saddened by yesterday evening's reported loss of life in a mosque in Gamboru Market, Maiduguri, in Borno State in north-east Nigeria, following a suspected suicide bombing attack.

News Reports

  • State Council meeting President of Russia 25 Dec 2025 -- Vladimir Putin chaired a meeting of the State Council on training professionals for the Russian economy.
  • Russia Has Plan to Respond to Possible Seizure of Assets Frozen in West - Deputy PM Novak Sputnik 25 Dec 2025 -- Russia has a plan to respond to the possible seizure of assets frozen in the West, and it will be implemented if necessary, Russian Deputy Prime Minister Alexander Novak said on Thursday.
  • Over 50 Satellites to Be Sent From Vostochny Cosmodrome in Final 2025 Launch - Roscosmos Sputnik 25 Dec 2025 -- Russian rocket Soyuz-2.1b will put 52 satellites into orbit from the Vostochny Cosmodrome located in Russia's eastern Amur Region during the final launch of 2025, Russian state space corporation Roscosmos said on Thursday.

  • Xi chairs CPC leadership meeting on Party conduct, anti-corruption work PRC MND 25 Dec 2025 -- The Political Bureau of the Communist Party of China (CPC) Central Committee convened a meeting on Thursday to discuss and plan the Party's efforts to improve conduct, build integrity, and combat corruption for the year 2026.
  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Lin Jian's Regular Press Conference on December 25, 2025 PRC MofA 25 Dec 2025
  • China opposes relevant country's irresponsible comments on China-India ties: FM Global Times 25 Dec 2025 -- China opposes relevant country's groundless and irresponsible comments on China-India ties, a Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson said on Thursday in response to claims made in a recent Pentagon report, which was also slammed by the Chinese Defense Ministry as being filled with erroneous perceptions of China and geopolitical biases.
  • Individual so-called human rights experts urged to stop making any unfounded criticisms on Jimmy Lai verdict: HKSAR government spokesperson Global Times 25 Dec 2025 -- Individual so-called human rights experts have entirely no respect for the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region (HKSAR) court's independent judgment, which had been made on the basis of facts and evidence, said a spokesperson of the HKSAR government on Wednesday, expressing dissatisfaction with and opposition to the unfounded comments after the court of the HKSAR convicted Lai Chee-ying (Jimmy Lai) of offences of endangering national security in strict accordance with the law and evidence.
  • Zhuque-3 reusable rocket may realize 'successful recovery' in 2026, says chief designer Global Times 25 Dec 2025 -- Dong Kai, deputy chief designer of the Zhuque-3 reusable rocket by Chinese private aerospace firm LandSpace, told the Global Times on Thursday that the rocket is expected to achieve a successful recovery by middle 2026, with plans to evolve into its "full configuration" within the same year.

  • Aircraft carrier Fujian's Taiwan Straits transit a normal training, more to be organized as needed: Defense Ministry spokesperson Global Times 25 Dec 2025 -- When asked to comment on the claims by Taiwan's "defense authority" that the aircraft carrier Fujian transited the Taiwan Straits on December 16, and that it would continue to conduct close monitoring, Zhang Xiaogang, a spokesperson at the Chinese Defense Ministry, said at a regular press conference on Thursday that this was a normal training conducted by the aircraft carrier Fujian, and that such training will continue to be organized as needed.
  • Taiwan patrol vessels monitor Chinese coast guard in Kinmen waters CNA 25 Dec 2025 -- Taiwan dispatched three patrol frigates to shadow and monitor Chinese coast guard vessels after the latter entered restricted waters off the Kinmen Islands, the Coast Guard Administration (CGA) said on Thursday.
  • Futile attempts made by US media to peddle 'war anxiety' will only undermine peace and stability across Taiwan Straits: MND spokesperson Global Times 25 Dec 2025 -- In response to recent US media reports hyping the Chinese People's Liberation Army (PLA) will "attack" the island of Taiwan in three steps, while claiming a so-called "optimal time" and "optimal landing sites" for the attack, a Chinese Ministry of National Defense (MND) spokesperson said on Thursday that the futile attempts made by certain media outlets to speculate about the PLA's operations and maliciously peddle "war anxiety" will only undermine peace and stability across the Taiwan Straits.
  • Those who seek to split the country will be nailed to the pillar of shame in history, says MND spokesperson on DPP's US-related remarks Global Times 25 Dec 2025 -- A spokesperson for China's Ministry of National Defense (MND) warned the DPP authorities on Thursday that those who betray the nation and attempt to split the country will inevitably be nailed to the pillar of shame in history.
  • Chinese PLA will not cease efforts to combat separatist activities, promote national reunification: spokesperson PRC MND 25 Dec 2025 -- China's defense ministry on Thursday said that the People's Liberation Army (PLA) will not cease its efforts to combat separatist activities and promote national reunification.
  • Taiwan 'open' to possibilities with Honduras after presidential poll CNA 25 Dec 2025 -- The Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MOFA) said Thursday it will maintain an "open and pragmatic" attitude toward future developments with Honduras in the wake of its presidential election in which the winner pledged to restore ties with Taiwan.
  • China respects Honduran people's choice for president, ready to advance ties based on one-China principle: FM Global Times 25 Dec 2025 -- In response to a media inquiry concerning that Honduras' National Electoral Council announced the vote count results on December 24 and declared National Party candidate Nasry Asfura the country's newly elected president, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lin Jian said China respects the choice of the Honduran people and stands ready to work with Honduras, on the basis of the one-China principle, to continue advancing bilateral relations.
  • China may see full attack as best unification option: Pentagon CNA 25 Dec 2025 -- Beijing could eventually see a full amphibious invasion of Taiwan as the only "prudent" way to bring about unification, the United States Department of Defense said in a newly released annual report to Congress.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 25 Dec 2025

  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un Receives Message of Greeting from Russian President KCNA 25 Dec 2025 -- Kim Jong Un , president of the State Affairs of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea, received a message of greeting from V. Putin, president of the Russian Federation, on Dec. 18.

  • Foreign Ministry's first mission is to work for lifting sanctions: Araghchi IRNA 25 Dec 2025 -- Minister of Foreign Affairs Abbas Araghchi says his ministry currently has two important missions, the first and most significant of which is the effort to lift sanctions.
  • Iran's enemies seeking to make up for military losses with economic warfare: FM Press TV 25 Dec 2025 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says Iran's enemies have shifted their pressure tactics towards economic warfare after failing to achieve their objectives through military means, targeting the livelihoods of ordinary Iranians.
  • West seeks to halt Iran's scientific progress: Nuclear chief IRNA 25 Dec 2025 -- Iran's nuclear chief says the West's core dispute with the Islamic Republic is not nuclear weapons but the country's scientific and technological advancement.
  • Israel targeted radiopharmaceutical facility in first missile strike on Isfahan: Iran's nuclear chief Press TV 25 Dec 2025 -- Israel's first missile strike on the city of Isfahan during the 12-day war targeted the city's fuel plate production facility for manufacturing radiopharmaceuticals, says Iran's nuclear chief.

  • PM Netanyahu speaks at the Conference for Senior Security and Foreign Affairs Officials Israel - Prime Minister 25 Dec 2025
  • 'Normalization not in Iraq's lexicon': PM Sudani vehemently rejects ties with Israel Press TV 25 Dec 2025 -- Iraqi Prime Minister Mohammed Shia' al-Sudani firmly rejected any prospect of diplomatic ties with Israel, stressing that normalization has no place in Iraq's political, legal, or moral framework.
  • Israel says another man arrested on suspicion of spying for Iran Press TV 25 Dec 2025 -- An Israeli man has been arrested on charges of conducting surveillance near former prime minister Naftali Bennett's home and passing information to Iranian intelligence.

  • Statement on the elections in Honduras Spain MFA 25 Dec 2025
  • Honduras: Statement by the Spokesperson on the general elections European External Action Service (EEAS) 25 Dec 2025
